claim: Model Context Protocol standardizes how LLMs receive external data: Tools (actions), Resources (raw context), and Prompts (templates)
actionable: When building LLM integrations, structure them as tools, resources, or prompts; this makes them portable across different AI applications

claim: MCP solves the copy-paste problem: avoid manually shuffling data between Claude, IDE, and other tools by standardizing how context flows to AI applications
actionable: Use MCP servers to eliminate manual copy-paste workflows; any API or data source can become a tool available to LLMs

claim: At Anthropic's hack week, engineers organically chose MCP for virtually all projects without mandate; proof that MCP solves real workflow friction
actionable: If a technology gets adopted organically across teams without mandate, it signals genuine value; use organic adoption as a validation signal

claim: Open-source specification approach reduces friction for server builders: they can build once and it works across all MCP-compatible clients automatically
actionable: Build integrations as open standards when possible; it multiplies adoption by eliminating client-specific implementations

claim: MCP is transitioning from local-only to cloud-hosted servers (Remote MCP), with Claude.ai integrations enabling web-based MCP servers as day-one use case
actionable: Publish MCP servers as web services; Remote MCP enables anyone to integrate them without local setup

claim: The community has built 10,000+ MCP servers organically; people are contributing, maintaining, and fixing issues without centralized coordination
actionable: Open-source standards with low friction attract large volunteer ecosystems; enable community contributions by removing barriers

claim: Upcoming MCP features: Registry API (models can search for servers on-demand), Long-running tasks, and Elicitation (servers ask users for info)
actionable: Expect future MCP servers to autonomously discover other servers and request clarification from users; plan for more agentic interactions

claim: With improved models (Claude 4, new Sonnet), primitives like statefulness and sampling become more used; models can now run longer-running agentic tasks
actionable: As models improve, leverage stateful MCP servers and multi-step task execution; previous bottlenecks become viable

claim: You can attach many MCP servers to Claude; overlap and tool naming matter—distinct tools work well together, overlapping tools can confuse the model
actionable: When combining multiple MCP servers, ensure clear naming and avoid overlapping functionality; name tools to reduce ambiguity
